channel.title: CTV News – Queen's Park
channel.description: Queen's Park coverage from CTV News Toronto.

item.title: NDP, Liberals speaking out after Ford government moves to host late-night session amid changes to FOI legislation
Ontario Liberals have first official leadership contender
item.description: The race to become the Ontario Liberals’ next leader has its first official entrant. Dylan Marando, a former political staffer, says on a website launching his bid that he wants to build an Ontario of inclusion, optimism and opportunity.

item.title: Ontario sets financial literacy requirement for high school graduation
item.description: Ontario high school students will soon need to pass a financial literacy test in order to graduate.

item.title: Doug Ford says private jet has been sold for same price
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford says the province has sold off a private jet it purchased for $28.9 million following an outcry.

item.title: Six mistakenly released inmates still at large, Ontario Premier Doug Ford says
item.description: Premier Doug Ford says six inmates mistakenly released from Ontario’s jails remain at large.

item.title: Doug Ford backs off government plane purchase, seeks to sell aircraft
item.description: Just days after announcing plans to buy a private jet, Ontario Premier Doug Ford says the province will now move to sell its newly acquired plane, acknowledging concerns about the timing and cost of the purchase.

item.title: Ontario forging ahead with medical residency rule for international grads it recently rescinded
item.description: Ontario is embedding in legislation a policy that would give priority for medical residency positions to applicants with a connection to the province.

item.title: Ford government to bypass public hearings on freedom-of-information clampdown
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford’s government is planning to skip public hearings to pass its omnibus budget bill that contains a retroactive clampdown on access to his cellphone records.

item.title: GO Train service returning to Stratford with daily trips to Toronto
item.description: Stratford is preparing for the return of GO Train service and city leaders say the move could provide a major boost to tourism in the Festival City.

item.title: ‘Terrible timing;’ Doug Ford facing opposition criticism over purchase of $28.9M private jet
item.description: A brand new 28.9 million private jet is taking off at Queen’s Park and so is the criticism across Ontario. A brand new 28.9 million private jet is taking off at Queen’s Park and so is the criticism across Ontario.

item.title: Doug Ford nixes idea of grocery surveillance pricing ban in Ontario
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford lamented Thursday that grocery store prices are sky high, but nixed the idea of banning so-called surveillance pricing and slammed a pilot project for city-run grocery stores as socialist and “crazy.”

item.title: ‘I’m absolutely furious’: Ford promises no more inmates will be improperly released from jail
item.description: A “furious” Premier Doug Ford has promised to keep all inmates behind bars after news broke that more than 150 of them were improperly released from jails across Ontario over the last few years.

item.title: Doug Ford vows to find out how Ontario’s jails improperly released more than 150 inmates
item.description: More than 150 inmates have been improperly released from Ontario’s jails over several years, a problem that Premier Doug Ford called “unacceptable” on Wednesday.

item.title: ‘If we can’t build peace in Ontario, where can we build it?’ Leaders speak out against rising antisemitism at Holocaust remembrance event
item.description: Leaders from all four elected parties at Queen’s Park took part in a ceremony Tuesday to mark Yom Hashoah, a day to remember the Holocaust, and took the occasion to speak out against increasingly violent acts of antisemitism.

item.title: Controversial Ontario fund rejected TTC training program with little explanation: documents
item.description: The Ontario government rejected a program that would have helped 21 high school graduates learn trades at Toronto’s transit system using a boilerplate letter with little explanation, documents obtained by CTV News show.
